fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to build out a fluent builder toolkit for our Places SDK wrapper. Basically devs keep complaining that constructing place objects and service request objects is super tedious and error-prone — they have to remember which fields are required vs optional every single time, and the boilerplate is everywhere. We want a single clean entry point per object type where you pass in the required stuff upfront and then configure anything optional in one trailing block.

There are two main groups of things to build: the data model side (photos, plus codes, address stuff, places, opening hours, predictions) and the service request side (fetch place, fetch photo, find current place, autocomplete, text search). The request objects also need to support cancellation — similar to how we handled it in that token-lifecycle pattern from the last SDK module — where you can prove the request is actually holding a live handle and not just a snapshot.

Also important: the codebase needs to be structured cleanly with the JSON adapter stuff kept totally separate from the core builder logic. No leaking of output/formatting concerns into the domain layer. We want this to be extensible so adding new object types later doesn't require touching existing code.

For location biasing on some requests you can do either a box (two corners) or a circle (center + distance). Price levels on text search have a specific internal integer mapping we need to handle correctly. Ping me if anything is unclear.

One small follow-up after the questions that came in: for photo-ish data, if no attribution is provided it must default to an empty string, not null and not 'none', and when width or height are omitted they must each default to integer 0. The output lines there are exactly attributions=<text>, width=<int>, height=<int>. On the address side, if the short name is missing from an address component, we should report the literal string 'none' in lowercase. So the line is short_name=none, not an empty string and not null.

Also wanted to pin down a couple formatting/details things so nobody has to guess. For place output, the order matters: first 'address=<value>', then 'address_component_count=<n>', then for each component at index i we print 'address_component[i].name=<name>', 'address_component[i].short_name=<short_name or none>', and 'address_component[i].types=[<type1>, <type2>, ...]'. For opening hours periods, it is valid for a period to have only a closing boundary and no opening boundary. If the opening boundary is absent, report the single line 'period[i].open=none'. If it exists, then it comes out as 'period[i].open.day=<DAY>' and 'period[i].open.time=HH:MM'. Time formatting should always be zero-padded HH:MM, so hour=0, minute=0 → '00:00' and hour=9, minute=5 → '09:05'.

On fetch-photo requests, if max_width or max_height are not set, they should each come through as the literal string 'none', specifically 'max_width=none' and 'max_height=none'. And on the cancellation side, yes, use the same CancellationTokenSource / CancellationToken pattern from the Background terminology section of start.md: the source owns the token handle, cancelling the source flips isCancellationRequested from false to true, and the request needs to hold the live handle rather than a copy so that flip is visible through the request object.

Last thing, just to make the architecture expectation extra explicit: the JSON parsing, command routing, and stdout formatting all need to live in a physically separate execution adapter layer. The core builder/construction logic should know nothing about JSON or stdout. We still want the split to stay clean between (1) data-model construction helpers and (2) service-request construction helpers, with (3) the adapter layer kept physically separate from both. And adding a new constructible kind should not require modifying existing builder code.

trigger_keywords: cancellation token live handle
prove request holds live token
cancellation state before and after
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When a cancellation token is attached to a request, the output must show 'cancellation_requested_before_cancel=false' then 'cancellation_requested_after_cancel=true'. The 'after' line is produced by actually cancelling the owning cancellation source after construction, proving the request holds the live handle and not a detached copy. When no token is attached, output 'cancellation_token=absent'.

trigger_keywords: session token reporting
how to confirm session token is attached
session token absent vs present
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For fetch-place requests: when a session token is attached the output line is 'session_token_matches=true' (confirming the same instance is wired in). When no session token is provided the line is 'session_token=absent'.

trigger_keywords: price level integer mapping
how price levels map to numbers
FREE INEXPENSIVE MODERATE EXPENSIVE VERY_EXPENSIVE values
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Price level names map to integer wire values as follows: FREE=0, INEXPENSIVE=1, MODERATE=2, EXPENSIVE=3, VERY_EXPENSIVE=4. The output reports the integer values in a list, e.g. price_levels=[2, 3] for MODERATE and EXPENSIVE.

trigger_keywords: location bias type rectangular vs circular
how to output location bias
rectangular bias corners circular bias center radius
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Rectangular bias outputs: 'location_bias.type=rectangular', 'location_bias.southwest=<lat>,<lng>', 'location_bias.northeast=<lat>,<lng>'. Circular bias outputs: 'location_bias.type=circular', 'location_bias.center=<lat>,<lng>', 'location_bias.radius=<meters>'. Coordinates are printed as decimal without trimming trailing zeros from the original value.

trigger_keywords: types_filter lowercase
autocomplete place type filter casing
how are type filters printed in autocomplete request
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: In the autocomplete predictions request, the types_filter values must be output in lowercase regardless of how they were supplied (e.g. input 'ESTABLISHMENT' → output 'establishment'). Country filters are output as-is (uppercase preserved).

trigger_keywords: text search optional fields
which fields are optional on search by text
search by text request with no extras
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A text-search request requires only text_query and place_fields. All other fields (included_type, is_open_now, min_rating, price_levels, rank_preference, region_code, location_bias) are optional and must be omitted from output entirely when not provided — do not output 'none' or 'absent' lines for them.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs

show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The open/closed architectural constraint in start.md §Architecture constraint 3: the set of constructible kinds must be open for extension without rewriting existing ones, implemented via an abstraction over 'construct and render' used by the high-level router rather than concrete switch/if-else dispatch on kind strings.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect. Please refer to the test case JSON files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact expected output format, and to the Architecture & Engineering Constraints section in start.md for structural requirements. If you can describe the business scenario you're trying to handle I can try to clarify further.

feature1_1_photo_metadata.json.description: Build photo metadata from a required photo reference, optionally setting attribution text and pixel dimensions. With no optional fields the dimensions default to zero and the attribution text defaults to empty. Output reports the resulting attribution text, width and height.

feature1_2_plus_code.json.description: Build a plus code object by assigning its compound code and global code. Output echoes both codes back.

feature1_3_address_component.json.description: Build an address component from a required name and a required list of type tags, with an optional short name. When the short name is omitted it is reported as absent. Output reports the name, short name, and types.

feature1_4_place.json.description: Build a place by assigning a free-form address line and a list of structured address components (each itself built from name, types and optional short name). Output reports the address, the number of attached components, and each component's name, short name and types.

cases.expected_output:
address=address
address_component_count=1
address_component[0].name=Main Street
address_component[0].short_name=Main St.
address_component[0].types=[street_address]

feature1_5_period.json.description: Build an opening-period by assigning its opening and closing moments. Each moment is a day-of-week plus a time-of-day (hour and minute). Output reports the day and the zero-padded hour:minute of each boundary.

feature1_6_opening_hours.json.description: Build an opening-hours object by assigning a list of opening-periods plus a list of human-readable weekday text lines. A period may set only a closing boundary, leaving the opening boundary absent. Output reports the period count, each period's boundaries, and the weekday text list.

feature1_7_autocomplete_prediction.json.description: Build an autocomplete prediction from a required place identifier and an optional list of place-type tags. Output reports the place identifier and the assigned place types.

feature2_1_fetch_place_request.json.description: Build a request to fetch a single place by its identifier and a list of requested fields. Optional extras: a cancellation token and a session token. When the cancellation token is requested, output shows the cancellation state flips from not-requested to requested after the source is cancelled, proving the same token instance is wired into the request. When the session token is requested, output confirms the same token instance is attached. When either extra is omitted it is reported as absent.

feature2_2_fetch_photo_request.json.description: Build a request to fetch a photo binary from its photo metadata, optionally bounding the returned image with a maximum width and height, and optionally attaching a cancellation token. The request echoes back the supplied photo metadata (attribution text, width, height). When the max-size bounds are omitted they are reported as absent. When the cancellation token is requested, output shows its state flips to requested after the source is cancelled.

cases.expected_output:
photo_metadata.attributions=
photo_metadata.width=0
photo_metadata.height=0
max_width=none
max_height=none
cancellation_token=absent

photo_metadata.attributions=
photo_metadata.width=0
photo_metadata.height=0
max_width=100
max_height=100
cancellation_requested_before_cancel=false
cancellation_requested_after_cancel=true

feature2_3_find_current_place_request.js.description: Build a request to find the device's approximate current place, given a list of requested fields and an optional cancellation token. Output reports the requested fields; when the cancellation token is requested it shows the cancellation state flips from not-requested to requested after the source is cancelled, otherwise it is reported as absent.

feature2_4_autocomplete_predictions_requ.description: Build a request for autocomplete predictions, configuring a free-text query, a list of country filters, a list of place-type filters, a rectangular location bias (defined by southwest and northeast corner coordinates), and a cancellation token. Output reports the query, country list, type filters, the rectangular bias corners, and the cancellation state flipping to requested after the source is cancelled.

cases.expected_output:
query=query
countries=[USA]
types_filter=[establishment]
location_bias.type=rectangular
location_bias.southwest=1.0,1.0
location_bias.northeast=2.0,2.0
cancellation_requested_before_cancel=false
cancellation_requested_after_cancel=true

feature2_5_search_by_text_request_basic..description: Build a text-search request from a query string and a list of requested fields, with no extra options. Output reports the query and the requested fields.

feature2_6_search_by_text_request_full.j.description: Build a fully-configured text-search request: query string, requested fields, an included place-type, an open-now flag, a minimum rating, a set of named price levels, a ranking preference, a region code, and a circular location bias (center coordinate plus radius in meters). The named price levels FREE/INEXPENSIVE/MODERATE/EXPENSIVE/VERY_EXPENSIVE map to integer wire values 0..4 respectively. Output reports each configured option, the price levels as their integer wire values, and the circular bias center and radius.

cases.expected_output:
text_query=test query
place_fields=[NAME, ADDRESS]
included_type=national_park
is_open_now=true
min_rating=4.0
price_levels=[2, 3]
rank_preference=RELEVANCE
region_code=US
location_bias.type=circular
location_bias.center=42.193893370553916,-122.7088890892941
location_bias.radius=1500.0
